title: Reading and Use of English – Part 1
description: For questions 1–8, read the text below and choose which answer (A, B, C or D) best completes each gap.

content.text: Artificial intelligence has (0) remarkable progress in recent years, transforming the way we live and work. From virtual assistants that respond to voice commands to algorithms that can diagnose diseases, AI is becoming increasingly (1) in our daily lives. Machine learning, a branch of AI, enables computers to learn from data without being explicitly programmed, allowing them to (2) patterns and make predictions. While some people are excited about the potential benefits, others have raised (3) about the impact on employment and privacy. Many routine jobs are at (4) of being automated, which could lead to significant changes in the labour market. However, experts point out that new technologies have always created new types of work, even as they made other jobs (5). The key challenge lies in ensuring that the benefits of AI are distributed fairly across society. Governments and companies must work together to (6) ethical guidelines for AI development. As this technology continues to evolve, it is (7) that we engage in open discussions about its implications. The decisions we make now will (8) the future of human-machine interaction for generations to come.

example.explanation: 'Made progress' is a fixed collocation to express progress or improvements.

questions.explanation: 'Present in' is the correct collocation to indicate that something is present or exists in a place.
'Identify patterns' is the correct technical collocation to describe pattern recognition.
'Raised concerns' is the correct formal expression to express concerns.
'At risk of' is a fixed expression that indicates the possibility of something negative happening.
'Obsolete' means that something is no longer useful or necessary, used for jobs eliminated by technology.
'Establish guidelines' is the correct formal collocation for creating official standards or guidelines.
'It is vital that' is an emphatic expression that indicates something of utmost importance.
'Shape the future' is a common collocation that means to influence what the future will be like.
